Self-Correction/Refinement on DP transition:

When calculating $C_i[V_{new}] = \sum_{V_{old} \in Map[D_{i-1}], V_{old} \le V_{new}} C_{i-1}[V_{old}]$.

If we use $DP_{prev}[V]$ to store $C_{i-1}[V]$, then the sum is simply the prefix sum up to $V_{new}$.

Let $S_{i-1}[V] = \sum_{k=0}^{V} DP_{prev}[k] \pmod{MOD}$.
Then $DP_{current}[V_{new}] = S_{i-1}[V_{new}]$.

This seems correct and robust.

Let's start coding. Remember to use BigInt if intermediate sums exceed $2^{53}-1$, but since we are applying modulo $10^9+7$ at every summation step, standard JavaScript numbers (which are IEEE 754 doubles, safe up to $2^{53}-1 \approx 9 \times 10^{15}$) should be fine, as $10^9+7$ squared is less than $10^{19}$. We will ensure modulo operations are applied correctly. $MOD$ is $10^9 + 7$.

Since the constraints $N \le 1000$ and $V_{max} \le 5000$ mean the total count can be up to $5001^{1000}$, we must use modulo arithmetic throughout.

$MOD = 1000000007$.
